Title: Dynamic metabolic and transcriptomic profiling of methyl jasmonate-treated hairy roots reveals synthetic characters and regulators of lignan biosynthesis in Isatis indigotica Fort

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinally active phenylpropanoid dimers in Isatis indigotica.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as a hub synthetic-associated gene in the lignan network, suggesting an important role in lignan formation, but showed negative correlations and was not functionally validated in this study.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Temporal RNA-seq profiling; co-expression network analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.5 µM methyl jasmonate elicitation in hairy roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DIR9 was one of only two synthetic-associated hub genes in the lignan co-expression network. 2. It showed negative correlations to other network elements. 3. Its putative role in lignan biosynthesis was suggested but not experimentally validated here.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRA SRP053268; untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-qTOF-MS
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 4CL3 and other network elements by correlation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for future functional analysis and possible lignan stereoselective engineering, but current evidence is predictive only.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dirigent proteins are associated with stereoselective coupling in lignan formation, but the paper did not biochemically validate DIR9 function.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glycosylated lignans may contribute to biological activity and storage.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted candidate lignan glucosyltransferase based on network presence and phylogenetic proximity to flax UGT74S1, but not experimentally validated.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.5 µM methyl jasmonate elicitation in hairy roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74F2 was one of only two UGT genes present in the lignan-associated network. 2. Phylogenetic analysis supported possible lignan glycosylation activity. 3. Function remains a prediction in this paper.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRA SRP053268; untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-qTOF-MS
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying lignan glycosylation and bioactive product composition after direct biochemical validation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed in Discussion as a candidate rather than experimentally established core enzyme.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glycosylated lignans may contribute to biological activity and storage.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted candidate lignan glucosyltransferase from network analysis; no direct functional validation reported.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.5 µM methyl jasmonate elicitation in hairy roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT72E1-2 appeared in the lignan-associated co-expression network. 2. It was proposed as a possible lignan glycosyltransferase. 3. No biochemical or genetic validation was provided.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRA SRP053268; untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-qTOF-MS
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for future engineering of lignan glycosylation profiles after validation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a network-predicted candidate only.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinally active phenylpropanoid dimers in Isatis indigotica.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Previously demonstrated dominant role in lignan synthesis in I. indigotica; mentioned here as prior evidence rather than newly tested core gene.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The discussion cites previous evidence that PLR1 has a dominant role in lignan synthesis in I. indigotica. 2. PLR1 was not the primary experimentally validated gene in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking target with 4CL3 for future lignan enhancement strategies, based on prior work.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included only as contextual gene supported by prior study, not a main experimental target of this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid branch feeding the major lignan route via caffeoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: caffeic acid; ferulic acid; cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids by ligating CoA to form the corresponding hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters, with strongest catalytic preference for caffeic acid in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinally active phenylpropanoid dimers in Isatis indigotica.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control point / putative rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignan accumulation; RNAi of 4CL3 significantly reduced lariciresinol/lignan production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression network analysis; subcellular localization; recombinant enzyme kinetics in Escherichia coli; RNAi knockdown in hairy roots; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in roots than stems and leaves; induced by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in roots than stems and leaves; induced by methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.5 µM methyl jasmonate elicitation in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL3 was identified as a hub synthetic gene in the MeJA-responsive co-expression network linked to lignan metabolism. 2. Recombinant 4CL3 showed highest affinity toward caffeic acid and also acted on ferulic and cinnamic acids. 3. RNAi silencing of 4CL3 reduced lariciresinol by about 49.3%, demonstrating a positive role in lignan biosynthesis. 4. The study highlighted a major lignan route in I. indigotica dominated by caffeic acid esterification catalyzed by 4CL3.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRA SRP053268; untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-qTOF-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate target for metabolic engineering to increase lignan production in I. indigotica; can be prioritized for overexpression or stacking with downstream lignan genes such as PLR1/PLR3, with attention to possible flux redistribution into other phenylpropanoid branches.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing different 4CL isoforms altered expression of other family members, indicating compensatory effects and possible phenylpropanoid flux redistribution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among three tested 4CL isoforms, only 4CL3 positively correlated with lariciresinol accumulation in vivo; study concludes the dominant route to lignans proceeds mainly through caffeic acid activation by 4CL3.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Hub regulator connecting two subclusters in the lignan co-expression network, implying specific importance in coordinating synthetic and regulatory modules.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinally active phenylpropanoid dimers in Isatis indigotica.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Multiple transcription factor genes and synthetic genes in the lignan network; specific direct targets not experimentally validated
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Correlated with multiple lignan synthetic/regulatory genes in network
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional regulation inferred from co-expression network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted positive/central regulator of lignan biosynthesis under MeJA response; no direct perturbation validation reported.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Temporal RNA-seq profiling; co-expression network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.5 µM methyl jasmonate elicitation in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH77 was identified as a hub TF in the MeJA-responsive co-expression network. 2. It connected two major subclusters, suggesting a central coordinating role. 3. It is proposed as a candidate regulator of lignan biosynthesis but lacks direct functional validation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRA SRP053268; untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-qTOF-MS
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Multiple TF genes and synthetic genes in co-expression network
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory engineering target for elevating lignan accumulation, but requires direct validation before deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reference annotation linked to auxin response (AT3G23690), but lignan-related role is inferred only from network topology.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Hub regulator connecting two subclusters in the lignan co-expression network, implying specific importance in controlling lignan-associated transcriptional programs.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinally active phenylpropanoid dimers in Isatis indigotica.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Multiple transcription factor genes and synthetic genes in the lignan network; specific direct targets not experimentally validated
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Correlated with multiple lignan synthetic/regulatory genes in network
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional regulation inferred from co-expression network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted central regulator of lignan biosynthesis under MeJA response; no direct perturbation validation reported.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Temporal RNA-seq profiling; co-expression network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.5 µM methyl jasmonate elicitation in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB18 was identified as a hub TF in the lignan-specific co-expression network. 2. It linked two network subclusters, indicating central regulatory importance. 3. It is a candidate regulator of lignan biosynthesis requiring further functional testing.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRA SRP053268; untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-qTOF-MS
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Multiple TF genes and synthetic genes in co-expression network
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential high-level engineering target to coordinately activate lignan pathway genes after validation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reference annotation linked to phytochrome A signaling (AT4G25560), but lignan-related role is inferred from network analysis only.
